params.name: RecordsPerPage
params.description: The number of records to return per page. This parameter is used for pagination. If not provided, a default value will be used.

params.name: Cursor
params.description: A cursor for pagination. This parameter is used to fetch the next set of results.

items.docs: Retrieves a paginated list of channels that the authenticated user has access to based on the provided access token.

params.name: Page
params.description: Page of orders to get

params.name: Limit
params.description: Amount of orders per page to request

params.name: IDs
params.description:
order ids to filter by, comma separated
Example: ?IDs=1,2

params.name: ReferenceIds
params.description:
Reference ids to filter by, comma separated
Example: ?ReferenceIds=Ref1,Ref2

params.name: StartDate
params.description: Start date to filter orders inserted later than

params.name: EndDate
params.description: End date to filter orders inserted earlier than

params.name: SortOrder
params.description: Order to sort results in

params.name: HasTracking
params.description: Has any portion of this order been assigned a tracking number

params.name: LastUpdateStartDate
params.description: Start date to filter orders updated later than

params.name: LastUpdateEndDate
params.description: End date to filter orders updated later than

params.name: IsTrackingUploaded
params.description: Filter orders that their tracking information was fully uploaded

params.name: LastTrackingUpdateStartDate
params.description: Start date to filter orders with tracking updates later than the supplied date. Will only return orders that have tracking information

params.name: LastTrackingUpdateEndDate
params.description: End date to filter orders updated later than the supplied date. Will only return orders that have tracking information

items.docs: Retrieves a paginated list of orders from your ShipBob account with optional filters. All filter parameters use AND logic, meaning orders must match all provided criteria to be returned.

Creates a new order in ShipBob’s system that will be queued for fulfillment. Orders can be created using either product reference IDs (SKUs) or ShipBob product IDs, with reference IDs being the recommended approach.
Retrieves detailed information about a specific order by its order ID, including order status, shipments, products, and recipient details.

params.name: shipmentId
params.description: The shipment id to get

items.docs: Retrieves detailed information about a specific shipment by shipment ID, including shipment status, tracking information, fulfillment center location, and contained products.

params.name: shipmentId
params.description: The shipment id to get

items.docs: Retrieves a chronological timeline of shipment status updates by shipment ID, showing when each status change occurred and providing visibility into the fulfillment progress.
Cancels multiple shipments in a single request. Shipments can only be cancelled if they are in a cancellable state (i.e. not picked, packed, or shipped yet).

Mark tracking information as synced to your external system for multiple shipments. Use this endpoint after syncing tracking data to update the IsTrackingUploaded flag, preventing these shipments from appearing in future GET /order queries with IsTrackingUploaded=false.

<Tip>Use this endpoint if you're filtering orders by is_tracking_uploaded. This allows integrations to acknowledge that shipments have been successfully synced to their system.</Tip>

items.docs: Updates the line items for a specific shipment. The request body must include the complete list of line items as returned by the GET /{shipmentId}:getLineItems endpoint — partial updates are not supported. Retrieve the current line items first, modify the desired fields, and submit the full payload.
Updates the shipping service for multiple shipments in a single request.

items.docs: Retrieves detailed shipment information for a specific order and shipment combination, including shipment status, tracking information, fulfillment center location, and contained products.

This endpoint will provide, where possible, an estimate of pricing and fulfillment center assignment of a potential standard (direct to consumer) order. Keep in mind that there are ways for the merchant to change FC assignment or product configuration after order creation that could invalidate this estimate. Estimates cannot be returned for items that are unknown, out of stock, or too large for fulfillment using standard box sizes. Additional services such as high-pick fees, shipping insurance,

params.name: Search
params.description: Search Products by name, sku, inventory id or product Id.

params.description: List of Category Ids associated with product

params.description: Looks for Products variants by their channel IDs

params.description: Looks for Products with/without digital variants

params.description: Looks for Products with/without variants

params.description: Looks for variants by its associated inventory id

params.description: Looks for Products variants by their IsInventorySyncEnabled is true

params.description: Looks for Products that have been updated since the given date

params.name: LegacyIds
params.description: Looks for Products with by Legacy Product Id(s)

params.name: Name
params.description: Looks for Products/Variants by name

params.name: OnHand
params.description: Looks for Products with inventory

params.description: Looks for Products variants by their external Platform IDs

params.description: Looks for Products with an assigned Id

params.description: Looks for Products by type

params.description: Looks for Products by ReviewsPending

params.description: Looks for Products variants assigned to a platform/sales channel

params.description: Looks for Products that match the provided Seller query

params.description: Looks for Products that match the provided Sku query

params.name: TaxonomyIds
params.description: Looks for Products variants by their taxonomy id or any descendants of the taxonomies sent separated by comma

params.description: Looks for Products with variants that contain provided status

params.description: Id, Name, Category, TotalOnHandQty

params.description: ASC,DESC

items.docs: Retrieves a paginated list of products. Supports filtering by search term, barcode, SKU, status, and other criteria.
Creates a new product with one or more variants. Requires a product name and at least one variant with a unique SKU.
Retrieves a product with all its variants and details.

params.name: productId
params.description: The product identifier

items.docs: Updates a product. Supports partial updates - only the fields provided will be updated. When updating variants through this endpoint, you must include the variant ID.

params.name: productId
params.description: The product identifier

items.docs: Deletes a bundle product by ID. Delete operation not allowed for non-bundles.

params.name: productId
params.description: The product id

items.docs: Retrieves all variants for a specific product.
Adds new variants to an existing product. The request body must be an array of variant objects, each requiring a unique SKU.
Updates one or more product variants. The request body must be an array of variant objects. Each variant must include the variant ID, and only the fields provided will be updated.

params.name: variantId
params.description: The ID of the variant to use as the merge target

items.docs: Merges multiple product variants into a target variant. Provide an array of variant IDs to merge into the target variant specified in the path. Inventory from the merged variants will be consolidated into the target variant.
Moves variants from their current product to the target product specified in the path. Provide an array of variant IDs to move.
Creates a new product and moves existing variants from their current products into it. Provide the new product details and variant IDs.
Retrieves all available packaging requirements that can be assigned to a product.
Retrieves all available product taxonomies for categorizing products.
Retrieves a specific taxonomy node and its details by taxonomy ID.
Retrieves the parent taxonomy node for a given taxonomy ID.
Converts an existing product variant into a bundle product. Provide the bundle definition specifying which variants and quantities make up the bundle.

params.name: SearchBy
params.description: Search is available for 3 fields: Inventory ID, Name, and SKU. Expected behavior for search by Inventory ID is exact match. Expected behavior for search by Inventory Name or SKU is partial match (consecutive characters, case insensitive).

params.name: InventoryIds
params.description: Comma-separated list of inventory IDs to filter results. Use this to retrieve inventory levels for specific inventory items only.

params.name: IsActive
params.description: Filter inventory levels by active status. Set to true to return only active inventory items, false for inactive items. Omit to return all items regardless of status.

params.name: IsDigital
params.description: Filter inventory levels by digital product status. Set to true to return only digital products, false for physical products. Digital products are items that don't require physical fulfillment.

params.name: PageSize
params.description: Number of inventory level items to return per page. Controls pagination size for the response.

params.name: SortBy
params.description: Sort results by field name. Default is ascending order. Prefix with '-' for descending order (e.g., '-total_on_hand_quantity' sorts by quantity descending). Multiple fields can be comma-separated.

items.docs: Retrieves current inventory levels across all locations. Returns available, on-hand, committed, and other quantity types. Supports filtering by inventory IDs and product attributes.
Retrieves the aggregated inventory levels for a specific inventory item across all locations, including available, on-hand, committed, and other quantity types.
Retrieves detailed information about a specific inventory item by its ID, including product details, variant information, and associated metadata.

params.name: SearchBy
params.description: Search is available for 3 fields: Inventory ID, Name, and SKU. Expected behavior for search by Inventory ID is exact match. Expected behavior for search by Inventory Name or SKU is partial match (consecutive characters, case insensitive).

params.name: FilterOperations
params.description: Advanced filtering operations. Apply multiple key-value filters to refine inventory results. Each filter operation contains a 'key' (field name) and 'rawValue' (filter value) to match.

params.name: InventoryIds
params.description: Comma-separated list of inventory IDs to filter results. Use this to retrieve information for specific inventory items only.

params.name: IsActive
params.description: Filter by active status. True returns only active inventory items, False returns only inactive items. Omit to return both.

params.name: IsDigital
params.description: Filter by digital product status. True returns only digital products (no physical fulfillment), False returns only physical products. Omit to return both.

params.name: PageSize
params.description: Number of items to return per page. Controls pagination size for the response.

params.name: SortBy
params.description: Sort results by field name. Default is ascending order. Prefix with '-' for descending order (e.g., '-name' sorts by name descending). Multiple fields can be comma-separated.

items.docs: Retrieves a paginated list of all inventory items. Supports filtering by active status, tags, and various product attributes. Use query parameters to search and filter results.

Fetches inventory movement events that change on-hand quantities within the ShipBob network. Returns events such as receiving/stow, order picks, adjustments, restocks, and attribute updates. Filter by inventory IDs, event category, date range, and facility. Supports cursor-based pagination for large result sets.

info.name: Get All Inventory Levels Grouped By Fulfillment Center

params.name: LocationType
params.description: Filter by location type. Valid values: 'hub', 'spoke', or 'lts'. Defaults to all locations if not specified.

params.name: LocationId
params.description: Filter by specific fulfillment center location ID. Use this to retrieve inventory levels for a particular fulfillment center.

params.name: SearchBy
params.description: Search is available for 3 fields: Inventory ID, Name, and SKU. Expected behavior for search by Inventory ID is exact match. Expected behavior for search by Inventory Name or SKU is partial match (consecutive characters, case insensitive).

params.name: InventoryIds
params.description: Comma-separated list of inventory IDs to filter results. Use this to retrieve location-grouped inventory levels for specific inventory items only.

params.name: IsActive
params.description: Filter inventory levels by active status. Set to true to return only active inventory items, false for inactive items. Omit to return all items regardless of status.

params.name: IsDigital
params.description: Filter inventory levels by digital product status. Set to true to return only digital products, false for physical products. Digital products are items that don't require physical fulfillment.

params.name: PageSize
params.description: Number of location-grouped inventory level items to return per page. Controls pagination size for the response.

params.name: SortBy
params.description: Sort results by field name. Default is ascending order. Prefix with '-' for descending order (e.g., '-name' sorts by name descending). Multiple fields can be comma-separated.

items.docs: Retrieves inventory levels for all inventory items grouped by fulfillment center location. Supports filtering by location type, location ID, and various product attributes.

info.name: Get Inventory Levels Grouped By Fulfillment Center

items.docs: Retrieves inventory levels for a specific inventory item broken down by fulfillment center location. Shows quantity breakdowns for each location where the inventory is stored.

info.name: Get All Inventory Levels Grouped By Lot

params.name: LocationId
params.description: Filter by specific fulfillment center location ID. Use this to retrieve lot-based inventory levels for a particular warehouse or distribution center.

params.name: SearchBy
params.description: Search is available for 3 fields: Inventory ID, Name, and SKU. Expected behavior for search by Inventory ID is exact match. Expected behavior for search by Inventory Name or SKU is partial match (consecutive characters, case insensitive).

params.name: InventoryIds
params.description: Comma-separated list of inventory IDs to filter results. Use this to retrieve lot-grouped inventory levels for specific inventory items only.

params.name: IsActive
params.description: Filter inventory levels by active status. Set to true to return only active inventory items, false for inactive items. Omit to return all items regardless of status.

params.name: IsDigital
params.description: Filter inventory levels by digital product status. Set to true to return only digital products, false for physical products. Digital products are items that don't require physical fulfillment.

params.name: PageSize
params.description: Number of lot-grouped inventory level items to return per page. Controls pagination size for the response.

params.name: SortBy
params.description: Sort results by field name. Default is ascending order. Prefix with '-' for descending order (e.g., '-lot_date' sorts by lot date descending). Multiple fields can be comma-separated.

items.docs: Retrieves inventory levels for all inventory items grouped by lot number. Enables tracking of inventory across multiple lots, including expiration dates and manufacturing batch information.

info.name: Get Inventory Levels Grouped By Lot

items.docs: Retrieves inventory levels for a specific inventory item broken down by lot number. Useful for tracking inventory by manufacturing lot, expiration date, or batch information.

Retrieves the distribution details for a specific receiving order within ShipBob's Inventory Placement Program (IPP).

<Warning>This endpoint is available only to accounts enrolled in ShipBob's Inventory Placement Program (IPP).</Warning>

params.name: Ids
params.description: The IDs of the returns to fetch. Accepts a comma-separated list of return IDs (e.g., 123,456,789).

params.description: Comma-separated list of return reference IDs (RMA numbers) to filter by.

params.name: Status
params.description: Comma-separated list of return statuses to filter by (e.g., AwaitingArrival,Arrived,Processing,Completed,Cancelled).

params.name: ReturnTypes

params.name: IncludeInactive
params.description: Whether the inactive locations should be included or not

params.name: ReceivingEnabled
params.description: Return all the receiving enabled locations

params.name: AccessGranted
params.description: Return all the access granted locations

items.docs: Retrieves physical locations across ShipBob's fulfillment network. An active location (`is_active=true`) is operational for fulfillment processes, including receiving inventory and processing returns. Access to locations varies: some are available to all merchants by default, while others require special approval. Use the `access_granted` parameter to determine if a location is available to the authenticated user, and `receiving_enabled` to confirm if the location accepts inventory.

params.name: Cursor
